name: Wei Yan (Wenchang)
page_title: Wei Yan (Wenchang) 魏延 (文長) [Han Xuan, Shu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
ranks: General Who Maintains Distant Lands in Peace, Governor of Hanzhong
Major of the Prime Minister, Inspector of Liangzhou (AD 227)
Advisor of the Front Army, General Who Conquers the West, Marquis of Nanzheng (AD 230)
novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	Wei Yan never killed Han Xuan to save Huang Zhong.(Han Xuan surrendered Changsha of his own accord.)
	In fact, there is no historic record of Wei Yan having ever been a general of Liu Biao or Han Xuan. He was originally a mere common soldier.
	Wei Yan served under Zhang Fei for a brief period before Liu Bei took Han Zhong, and was highly respected by Zhang Fei for his intelligence.
	Wei Yan did not slay the fictional Cao Zun.
	Wei Yan did not slay Wang Shuang historically.

full_text:
Wei Yan (Wenchang) 魏延 (文長)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Wei Yan (魏延), styledWenchang (文長), was born in Yi Yang City (Presently Tong Bo in He Nan Province). Talented commander. Killed Han Xuan and surrendered his territory to Liu Bei. Wei Yan was affiliated with the Han Xuan Dynasty and the Shu Kingdom. Wei Yan lived during the Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.
Return to theThree Kingdoms Encyclopediaor browse theEncyclopedia Directory.

Short Biography
Talented commander. Killed Han Xuan and surrendered his territory to Liu Bei.
Details
Traditional Chinese魏延 (文長)
Simplified Chinese魏延 (文长)
PronunciationWei Yan (Wenchang)
Wade-GilesWei Yen (Wên-ch‘ang)
BirthplaceYi Yang City
(Presently Tong Bo in He Nan Province)
Rank and Titles
General of the Standard
General Who Maintains Distant Lands in Peace, Governor of Hanzhong
General Who Suppresses the North
Marquis of a Chief Village (AD 223)
Major of the Prime Minister, Inspector of Liangzhou (AD 227)
Advisor of the Front Army, General Who Conquers the West, Marquis of Nanzheng (AD 230)
Fact vs Fiction
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
Wei Yan never killed Han Xuan to save Huang Zhong.
(Han Xuan surrendered Changsha of his own accord.)
In fact, there is no historic record of Wei Yan having ever been a general of Liu Biao or Han Xuan. He was originally a mere common soldier.
Wei Yan served under Zhang Fei for a brief period before Liu Bei took Han Zhong, and was highly respected by Zhang Fei for his intelligence.
Wei Yan did not slay the fictional Cao Zun.
Wei Yan did not slay Wang Shuang historically.
Literary Appearances
Romance of the Three Kingdoms:41,53,55–56,60–65,70–72,81,85–97,99–104,105
